title: Debug and fix critical image loading failure for all 120 gallery images

requirements.text: Audit and verify that all 120 gallery image files (residential-1.png through residential-40.png, commercial-1.png through commercial-40.png, before-after-1.png through before-after-40.png) physically exist as static assets in the frontend/public/assets/generated directory with correct filenames and valid image file formats

quotes: Please recheck all the errors, file paths and correct the errors

acceptanceCriteria: All 120 image files exist in frontend/public/assets/generated directory
All filenames match the expected pattern (residential-1.png to residential-40.png, commercial-1.png to commercial-40.png, before-after-1.png to before-after-40.png)
All image files are valid PNG format and can be opened
File sizes are reasonable (not 0 bytes or corrupted)

requirements.text: Fix the Portfolio component image path construction to correctly reference all 120 gallery images using the absolute path pattern '/assets/generated/{category}-{number}.png' where category is 'residential', 'commercial', or 'before-after' and number ranges from 1 to 40

quotes: Please recheck all the errors, file paths and correct the errors

acceptanceCriteria: Image paths in Portfolio component use correct absolute path format starting with /assets/generated/
No relative path references (no ./ or ../ prefixes)
Category names match directory structure exactly (residential, commercial, before-after)
Number formatting is consistent (1-40, no zero-padding unless directory uses it)
All 120 images render correctly in the browser without 404 errors

requirements.text: Enhance error logging in the Portfolio component to capture and display detailed debugging information for each failed image load, including the complete attempted URL, HTTP status code, network error details, and CORS headers if applicable
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs show the exact URL attempted for each failed image
Error messages include HTTP status codes (404, 403, 500, etc.)
Network errors are caught and logged with full error details
CORS-related errors are specifically identified and logged
Each failed image load includes the image index and category for easy tracking

requirements.text: Verify the Vite/build configuration correctly serves static assets from the frontend/public directory and that the /assets/generated/ path is accessible at runtime without being transformed or relocated during the build process

quotes: Please recheck all the errors, file paths

acceptanceCriteria: Static assets in frontend/public are served at root level (public/assets/generated becomes /assets/generated)
Build process does not rename, move, or hash files in the generated directory
Assets are accessible in both development and production builds
No build warnings or errors related to asset handling

requirements.text: Regenerate any missing, corrupted, or improperly formatted gallery images among the 120 total images, ensuring all images are valid PNG files with appropriate dimensions (minimum 800x600 pixels) and represent high-quality interior design photography
acceptanceCriteria: All 120 gallery images are regenerated if any are missing or corrupted
All images are valid PNG format with appropriate file headers
Images have minimum dimensions of 800x600 pixels
Images represent diverse interior design styles and spaces as specified in original requirements
File sizes are reasonable (typically 100KB-2MB per image)

constraints: Do not change the backend Motoko actor or contact form storage logic
Maintain the existing three-category gallery structure (residential, commercial, before-after)
Preserve all existing Portfolio component functionality including filtering and animations
Keep image files in frontend/public/assets/generated directory
Use PNG format for all gallery images
nonGoals: Adding new gallery categories beyond residential, commercial, and before-after
Creating an admin interface for managing gallery images
Adding image optimization or lazy loading beyond what exists
Changing the number of images per category (keep at 40 each)
